title: Data Investigator
content:
Global Witness are looking for a Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and support the delivery of a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.

You will work with the Senior Data Investigator to help develop the core database underpinning the project’s public-facing map, and support data analysis and investigations that uncover the environmental, financial and public interest issues linked to abandoned oil and gas wells.

You will also contribute to the wider work of the Data Investigations Team, supporting the development and delivery of other data-led investigations, strengthening data investigation practice across the organisation, and helping ensure that data is used effectively in Global Witness’s publications and advocacy.

See vacancy
snippet: Global Witness are looking for a Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and support the delivery of a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Senior Data Investigator
content:
Global Witness are looking for a Senior Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and play a leading role in delivering a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.

You will lead the development of the core database underpinning the project’s public-facing map, and carry out rigorous data analysis and investigations that help uncover the environmental, financial and public interest issues linked abandoned to oil and gas wells.

You will also contribute to the wider work of the Data Investigations Team, supporting the development and delivery of other data-led investigations, strengthening data investigation practice across the organisation, and helping ensure that data is used effectively in Global Witness’s publications and advocacy.

See vacancy
snippet: Global Witness are looking for a Senior Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and play a leading role in delivering a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Earth Journalism Network Editorial Assistant
content:
Reporting to the EJN Senior Program Officer, the Editorial Assistant will be based in the UK and work with the EJN global team. The Editorial Assistant will be responsible for supporting the editorial and communications team with copywriting, content production and editorial tasks for the EJN website, newsletter and social media channels. They will also support with programmatic work including reviewing grant applications, onboarding grantees and administrative tasks.

The Editorial Assistant will be comfortable with both working independently and liaising between the various EJN teams and personnel working around the world, which will occasionally require joining calls and meetings during evening hours. This is a full-time position expected to start in May 2026 and last for an estimated 10 months, with the possibility of being extended.

See vacancy
snippet: Reporting to the EJN Senior Program Officer, the Editorial Assistant will be based in the UK and work with the EJN global team. The Editorial Assistant will be responsible for supporting the editorial
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Research and Outreach Officer (India)
content:
The Research and Outreach Officer will play a supporting role across the design, coordination, and implementation of research and outreach activities under the Break the Chain project, for strengthening local information systems in India. Working closely with senior team members, the role will help translate project plans into effective field execution by supporting research activities, coordinating stakeholder engagement, and ensuring smooth delivery of on-ground interventions. The Officer will assist in operationalising research designs, coordinating fieldwork, supporting data collection and presentation, coordinating with teams dealing with social listening, while also facilitating outreach with community members, local media, and partner organisations.

The role involves strong coordination, ensuring timely implementation, addressing field-level challenges, and maintaining quality and ethical standards. Overall, this position is suited for someone who is organised, proactive, and comfortable working at the intersection of research and field engagement, with the ability to support end-to-end execution and contribute to effective programme delivery. creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Writer
content:
Our World in Data is looking for an experienced writer. The ideal candidate already writes single-authored pieces regularly and publicly for a non-specialist audience, builds arguments based on data and research, and has the range to cover many of the topics they work on. This is a senior role: the person hired will work closely with Hannah Ritchie, their Deputy Editor. The hiring process involves a paid writing residency before any full-time offer.

See vacancy

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Become Your Newsroom’s Digital Security Expert
content:
Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide.

Digital threats to journalists are no longer theoretical. Doxing, phishing, compromising devices, and source exposure are daily realities and newsrooms usually lack of someone dedicated to security. Or they do have someone, but this person lacks resources and training. This intensive, two-session workshop transforms a motivated journalist into their newsroom’s go-to digital security resource, combining threat modeling, hands-on tool practice, and a train-the-trainer approach.

Dates: June 6 – June 7, 2026

Time: 8:00 – 10:00 am EDT; two hours per session (four hours total)

Format: Live online (interactive)

Participants: 22  |  Level: Basic (no technical b
snippet: Note: On-demand courses are fee-based, allowing us to sustainably support our team and trainers while continuing to provide free, high-quality investigative journalism training opportunities worldwide
creator: GIJN Staff

title: The Hidden Scenario: Building Timelines for Investigations
content:
The creator of Sherlock Holmes, Arthur Conan Doyle, was a trained physician, and studied under one of the great diagnosticians of his time. A proper medical diagnosis, as it happens, proceeds from a hypothesis that is verified by clinical examination and the patient’s history. That’s surely where Doyle’s description of Sherlock’s method came from: “The ideal reasoner would, when he had once been shown a single fact in all its bearings, deduce from it not only all the chain of events which led up to it but also all the results which would follow from it.”

Our work as investigative reporters isn’t fiction, but there are fictions we can learn from. If a hypothesis is the starting point of an investigation — the provisional answer to the question “what do we think happened?” — a timeline and a source map determine the path to verify the answer. By seeking when events occurred, where they occurred, and who was involved, we learn how things occurred.

Composing a timeline

creator: Mark Lee Hunter

title: Del caos a la claridad: Automatización de prompts para grandes conjuntos de datos
content:
REGISTRATE AQUÍ

Este taller en línea gratuito ofrece una introducción práctica y dinámica al uso de la inteligencia artificial aplicada al periodismo de investigación, con un enfoque en potenciar el trabajo con información a gran escala.

Los y las participantes aprenderán a extraer, clasificar y estructurar información de forma automatizada y a gran escala mediante el uso de APIs de inteligencia artificial, aprovechando la capacidad de comprensión semántica de los modelos para acelerar y profundizar procesos de investigación.

Desde cero, se enseñará qué es y cómo crear una API key, cómo interactuar con la API sobre grandes volúmenes de documentos o registros y cómo ajustar parámetros clave del modelo para obtener mejores resultados.

El enfoque es

creator: Reed Richardson

title: How to Tell the Stories of People Behind Data Points
content:
Investigative reporters encounter a common sourcing challenge after securing the data and the expert input on discrimination and inequity stories. How do they actually identify individual people experiencing the harms represented by the data points to humanize the piece — and how do they get them to talk? It’s a problem partly because the people behind the numbers tend to be new to media interaction, and often new to statistics, and don’t come forward on their own. And partly because the individuals most impacted by unjust policies — whether from a biased social security algorithm in South Africa to climate change impacts in Brazil — often don’t know that they are losing out relative to others.

In a session at the NICAR26 data journalism summit, a panel of veteran data reporters shared tips on how to amplify and contextualize data-driven publi
snippet: Investigative reporters encounter a common sourcing challenge after securing the data and the expert input on discrimination and inequity stories. How do they actually identify individual people exper
creator: Rowan Philp

title: Following the Money Fueling Environmental Destruction in War: A Case Study from Sudan
content:
In fragile, conflict-ridden states like Sudan, environmental harm is a byproduct of the economic strategies. When companies or state-linked entities avoid the high costs of treating industrial pollution or waste, their saved money eventually acts as an unreported subsidy that journalists can trace to understand why pollution is permitted to continue.

Traditional investigative processes often fail in places like Sudan due to the absence of functioning regulators and the perilous situation on the ground​.

While we usually think of a subsidy as a direct payment from a government to a business, this hidden subsidy is an indirect benefit gained by shifting the costs of pollution onto the public.

The core investigative question for reporters is: Who profits from the absence of regulation? There is no doubt that responsibility is often obscured by conflict, but
snippet: In fragile, conflict-ridden states like Sudan, environmental harm is a byproduct of the economic strategies. When companies or state-linked entities avoid the high costs of treating industrial polluti
creator: Mathani Ahmed

title: Five Questions on How Independent Hungarian Media Beat Orban’s ‘Propaganda Machine’
content:
After returning to power in Hungary 16 years ago, Viktor Orbán and his party, Fidesz, started taking the country’s free press apart piece by piece, writing a new playbook for how to bring journalism under the heel of the state.

“I wouldn’t even call it reporting because what [Orbán-controlled state media] did over the last 16 years wasn’t journalism. It was basically a political operation.” — Direkt36 co-founder András Pethö

But the propaganda machine he created was not enough to protect the right-wing prime minister indefinitely: Orbán was recently defeated in a landslide election win by Péter Magyar, who has pledged to suspend biased state media broadcasts and pass a new media law that ensures objective and impartial reporting.

As Hungary heads into this ne

creator: OCCRP

title: Editor
content:
We are looking for an experienced editor to join our award-winning team at Balkan Insight, BIRN’s flagship English-language publication, and help shape the future of independent journalism in the Balkans.

Balkan Insight is the leading English-language news and investigative website covering Southeast Europe, produced by the largest independent media network in the region.

The selected candidate will play a central role in managing editorial production and maintaining the highest possible journalistic standards.

See vacancy
snippet: We are looking for an experienced editor to join our award-winning team at Balkan Insight, BIRN’s flagship English-language publication, and help shape the future of independent journalism in the Balk
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Senior Editor, Research
content:
We are seeking a senior editor to lead ProPublica’s growing research team, guiding high-impact investigative work across the newsroom while supporting rigorous, collaborative and responsive research at scale.

The research team plays a critical role in many of ProPublica’s most impactful and widely read stories, contributing deep reporting, verification and sourcing to both long-term investigations and fast-turn accountability journalism. Research reporters regularly earn co-bylines and contributor credits across text, audio and video projects, and they collaborate closely with reporters, editors and partner newsrooms across the country.

Researchers typically balance two to three long-term projects while fielding short-term needs, providing expertise in public records, financial and government research, open-source intelligence and advanced verification.

This role reports to the assistant managing editor, national. You’ll lead ProPublica’s seven-member research team that works collaboratively across our national, local, Texas and Local Reporting Network desks.
snippet: We are seeking a senior editor to lead ProPublica’s growing research team, guiding high-impact investigative work across the newsroom while supporting rigorous, collaborative and responsive research a
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Engagement Reporter
content:
Engagement reporters do ProPublica’s signature crowdsourced investigations, using everything from callouts and tip lines to citizen-fueled science. As an engagement reporter, you’ll team up with colleagues across the country to gather evidence and receipts for hard-hitting, community-driven stories. You may also work with external partners on long- and short-term projects through our Local Reporting Network. You’ll find and reach people who have important stories to tell, figure out how to include them in the reporting process and work on journalism that could help improve their lives. You’ll likely come across more stories and new leads in the process.

Like everyone in our newsroom, our team focuses on accountability journalism and measures success by impact. We’ve worked with communities to tell thousands of people’s stories, including educators, parents, mental health providers, wildfire survivors, migrant dairy farm workers, vulnerable workers and residents living near toxic hot spots. We’ve filled information gaps with calculators, guides and letter generators. And we’ve done our best to reach people in the spaces where they gather, both online and off. This journali
snippet: Engagement reporters do ProPublica’s signature crowdsourced investigations, using everything from callouts and tip lines to citizen-fueled science. As an engagement reporter, you’ll team up with colle
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Deputy Director
content:
Index is an independent investigative NGO founded in 2020, specializing in open-source intelligence (OSINT), audiovisual analysis, and 3D reconstruction. Its work focuses primarily on police violence and human rights violations in France. A member of the Investigative Commons network and affiliated with Forensic Architecture, Index has covered major cases of police violence over the past decade and produced investigations recognized for their methodological rigor.

The Deputy Director is responsible for the operational management of Index. This is a cross-functional role with a strong focus on development. The successful candidate will manage the day-to-day operations of the organization and become the primary driver of its resource growth.

This recruitment is strategic: it will allow the Managing Director to refocus on editorial direction, survey production, and research, by entrusting operational management, resource development, and communication to a trusted individual.

See vacancy
snippet: Index is an independent investigative NGO founded in 2020, specializing in open-source intelligence (OSINT), audiovisual analysis, and 3D reconstruction. Its work focuses primarily on police violence
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Investigative Journalist
content:
ICIJ is looking for a seasoned investigative reporter to join the team. This role will be responsible for researching, writing, and editing independent investigative stories that hold the powerful to account and that uncover broken systems, incisively and in the public interest. It is an opportunity to independently investigate some of the most important topics of our time, to tell stories that bring startling truths to light, to optimize analysis and coverage, and to do so with a fiercely committed, award-winning team.

ICIJ is a nonprofit online media organization with headquarters in Washington, D.C.  The core of ICIJ’s work is the coordination of major journalism investigations that cover stories that cross borders, using the latest technology to facilitate those collaborations. ICIJ is an award-winning organization including a Pulitzer Prize and a Nobel Prize nomination. ICIJ is dedicated to building a culturally diverse and pluralistic staff committed to teaching and working in a multicultural environment.

See vacancy
snippet: ICIJ is looking for a seasoned investigative reporter to join the team. This role will be responsible for researching, writing, and editing independent investigative stories that hold the powerful to
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Head of Investigations
content:
The Head of Investigations role is an exciting opportunity for an experienced investigative journalist to get involved in a new, impact-focused project from the very beginning. The project will bring together investigative journalism and advocacy work focused on issues related to chemical use in the UK. It will focus on chemical harms to both human health and environment and dig deeper into the influence of the chemicals industry.

Working closely with the project’s Founder in a team of two, the Head of Investigations is a varied role which includes designing, conducting and publishing investigations on issues related to UK chemicals. The successful candidate will also co-lead multi-disciplinary project teams comprised of investigative journalists and NGO policy experts.

It’s a unique chance for an investigative journalist that’s passionate about preventing harms to health and environment to see their work have real-world impact.

Pesticide Action Network UK (PAN UK) is an independent, non-profit organisation, which works nationally and internationally to eliminate the use of the most hazardous pesticides and promote sustainable alternatives. The organisation

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Introduction to AI for Data-Driven Investigations
content:
This workshop offers a practical introduction to using AI for investigative journalism, focusing on real-world reporting applications. It covers workflows for extracting structure from text, cleaning data, identifying patterns, and checking findings with greater speed and depth, with demonstrations drawn from reporting on audit reports, public budgets, climate spending, and ad library data. It shows how investigative journalists can use AI tools to explore complex information and develop story ideas.

Date: May 21, 2026

Time: 9:30 am EDT

Register for the Zoom training here.

Trainer’s bio: Jaemark Tordecilla is a journalist, media a

creator: Reed Richardson

title: How to Verify Bystander Video
content:
Bystander and surveillance videos keep driving major news stories.

The Star Tribune’s work on the Good and Pretti shootings is a case in point. A team of visual journalists deconstructed bystander footage frame by frame, directly contradicting claims made by members of the Trump administration about both killings.

The killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti by US federal officers in Minneapolis. Brutal takedowns of lawful citizens and aggressive clashes by Immigration and Customs Enforcement across the US. The
snippet: Bystander and surveillance videos keep driving major news stories. The Star Tribune’s work on the Good and Pretti shootings is a case in point. A team of visual journalists deconstructed bystander foo
creator: Alex Mahadevan, Poynter

title: Hungary’s Historic Election, Artemis II’s Lunar Journey, Strait of Hormuz Shipping Crisis, and Three Years of War in Sudan
content: After completing NASA’s first crewed fly-by of the Moon in more than 50 years, the Artemis II team returned to Earth — drawing worldwide fascination, particularly from Australia’s ABC, which took readers on a visual journey through the mission and the space milestones that preceded it. Our roundup of the Top 10 in Data Journalism considers stories from March 30 to April 15, and also features Atlatszo’s live-tracking of the seismic Hungarian election that ended Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule, the South China Morning Post’s reconstruction of a deadly Hong Kong housing fire, the Guardian’s look at how Earth’s orbit is becoming crowded with satellites, and Sky News’s assessment of Russian dominance in the Arctic.
snippet: After completing NASA’s first crewed fly-by of the Moon in more than 50 years, the Artemis II team returned to Earth — drawing worldwide fascination, particularly from Australia’s ABC, which took read
creator: Hanna Duggal
